notes.author: run-scenario-agent
notes.text: Verified the "none-survive" outcome from content/scenario_plans/scenario2.json. Ran 4× STAYER_BOT on map scenario2. Result: survivors=0 (matches expected_survivors=0), complete=false (expected — the 0-survivor branch skips ctx.terminate/announceFact, so the scenario ends via timeout). scenario_summary: 'scenario "scenario2" timed out after 1250ms; 0/4 bots survived.' (run_artifact_id scenario2/all-stayers/1). Note: server reported effectiveTimeout=1250ms rather than the requested 15000ms — likely an infra clamp — but it didn't affect the verdict since stragglers were eliminated well before that. Goal met.

notes.author: run-scenario-agent
notes.text: Re-ran against new termination payload (run_artifact_id scenario2/all-stayers/2). Both assertions pass: (1) complete==true — scenario2 self-terminated via ctx.terminate() in eliminateStragglers, no longer hitting the 15000ms timeout; (2) survivor_count==0 as read from termination_metadata.final_state.survivor_count in the server-authored response.json (scenario_summary: "scenario \"scenario2\" completed; 0/4 bots survived."). Concrete log evidence: bot 0 client_logs include {level:"warn", message:"eliminated by server"} at t=1777031158481 (~800ms after welcome), followed by "stopped"; disconnected=true. All four stayer bots were eliminated by the server, then the scenario self-completed.
